title: AWS access keys should not be used directly in code
description: This rule checks if AWS access keys are hard-coded in the source code. This practice can lead to unauthorized access and data leaks.
message: AWS access key found hard-coded in the source code.
recommendation: Store AWS credentials in environment variables or use AWS SDK's secure token service.

noncompliant.description: Hard-coded AWS access key and secret key.

compliant.description: AWS access keys fetched from environment variables.

why_is_this_an_issue: Hard-coded access keys can lead to unauthorized access and data leaks.
how_to_fix: Store AWS credentials in environment variables or use AWS SDK's secure token service.

metadata.title: AWS access keys should not be used directly in code
